film.title: The Story of Temple Drake
film.overview: The coquettish granddaughter of a respected small-town judge is stranded at a bootleggers’ hide-out, subjected to an act of nightmarish sexual violence, and plunged into a criminal underworld that threatens to swallow her up completely.

dialogues.dialogue: I've seen three generations of 'em in my time.  They're stiffneck like most of 'em.  Proud and all that.  There's a wild streak in 'em.  Every now and then when often comes along like Temple.
He died serving his country in the World War?  Yes.  You're proud of your family, aren't you Temple?  I object your honor.  I don't see the point of all this.
dialogues.cause_raw: family's service in the World War

dialogues.dialogue: Don't you, Miss Drake?  Yes, I guess so.  You are proud of your family, aren't you?  Naturally.  Proud of that courage?
You are proud of your family, aren't you?  Naturally.  Proud of that courage?  Their willingness to make sacrifices?  Their... Their love of the truth?
dialogues.cause_raw: for family's courage sacrifice and love of truth

dialogues.dialogue: You can't.  You'll never find him.  I killed him.  Be proud of her, Judge.  I am.  nan
